name: Mixtile Blade 3
description: RK3588 Pico-ITX SBC with U.2 stacking, dual 2.5GbE, and PCIe Gen3 x4 — designed for compute clusters

release-date.comment: Crowd Supply campaign opened March 2022 with shipments targeted for late October 2022; User Manual v1.0 dated 2022-06-13.

status.comment: All four SKUs (4/8/16/32GB RAM) listed on the Mixtile store as of 2026-04-26; User Manual v1.3 was updated 2024-05-08.

open-source-hardware.comment: Vendor publishes a user manual and block diagram but no PCB design files or schematics under an OSHW-compatible license.

country-of-origin.comment: Mixtile is headquartered in Shenzhen, China; the company is registered as Mixtile Limited (Hong Kong).

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Block diagram lists Cortex-A76 up to 2.4 GHz, Cortex-A55 up to 1.8 GHz.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G610 supports OpenGL ES 3.2 via the vendor BSP; Panthor mainline driver also supports the same level.

hw-video-decode.comment: User Manual: H.265/H.264/VP9 decode up to 8K@60fps. AV1 decode is supported on RK3588 silicon but Mixtile does not list it.

hw-video-encode.comment: H.264/H.265 encode up to 8K@30fps. RK3588 has no AV1 or VP9 hardware encoder.

ram-type.comment: User Manual v1.3 lists "LPDDR4/LPDDR5". Earlier marketing material listed LPDDR4 only; later SKUs may use LPDDR5.

emmc.comment: Onboard eMMC, 32 GB / 64 GB / 128 GB / 256 GB SKUs.

microsd-slot.comment: Standard microSD socket (SDMMC 3.0, up to 64 GB per User Manual).

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No native M.2 connector. NVMe is reachable via the U.2 connector and an optional U.2-to-M.2 adapter case sold separately.

sata.comment: Native SATA 3.0 signals are exposed on the U.2 edge connector (not via USB-to-SATA).

ethernet.comment: Two RJ45 ports backed by Realtek RTL8125BG-CG PHYs, 2.5 GbE each.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi. The Mini-PCIe socket can host a third-party Wi-Fi/BT card.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth (paired with Wi-Fi absence).

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: No USB Type-A ports — host USB is exposed via USB-C only.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB0/ADB and USB1/PD: both USB 3.2 Gen 1 with DisplayPort 1.4a Alt-Mode; USB1/PD doubles as the USB-C PD power input.

hdmi-outputs.comment: One HDMI 2.1 OUT (8K@60 / 4K@120). A second HDMI 2.0 connector is an INPUT (4K@60), not counted as an output.

max-display-resolution.comment: User Manual: 7680×4320@60Hz over HDMI 2.1; DisplayPort 1.4a over USB-C tops out at 7680×4320@30Hz.

displayport.comment: DisplayPort 1.4a is exposed over USB-C DP-Alt on both Type-C ports; no native DP connector on the board.

mipi-dsi.comment: User Manual block diagram and product overview list only a 4-lane MIPI-CSI FPC connector. Some press articles (Hackster, ameriDroid) refer to a MIPI-DSI connector, but this appears to be a confusion with the MIPI-CSI FPC; primary sources do not corroborate it.

mipi-csi.comment: 4-lane MIPI-CSI FPC connector on the underside of the PCB.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack. Audio is exposed only as I2S signals on the 30-pin GPIO header (and over HDMI/DP digital).

gpio-pins.comment: 30-pin connector exposing digital I/O, I²C, USB 2.0, TTL UART, SPI, I²S, ADC, CAN. (One Mixtile docs page mistakenly says "40-pin"; the User Manual, the dedicated 30-pin header doc URL, and product photos all confirm 30 pins.)

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 30-pin pinout, not the Raspberry Pi 40-pin layout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pico-ITX form factor with a 30-pin header — incompatible with RPi HAT mechanical and electrical specs.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 4× PCIe 3.0 via U.2 edge connector + 1× PCIe 2.1 via Mini-PCIe socket
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: U.2 also carries SATA 3.0 and a 12 V power input; Mini-PCIe additionally exposes USB 2.0.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: All three (plus I²S, CAN, ADC) on the 30-pin header; a separate UART debug header is also broken out.

power-input.value: USB-C PD 2.0 (max 20V/3A) or 12V DC via U.2

idle-power-w.comment: Measured ~4.5 W at idle in The DIY Life review (16 GB / 128 GB SKU, USB-C PD 15 V input).

load-power-w.comment: Measured 7–7.5 W under load in the same review. Mixtile lists ≤10 W as the max.

cooling-required.comment: Ships with a heatsink pre-installed; ~7–8 W sustained load fits passive cooling. A fan header is provided for users who push it harder.

rtc-battery-header.comment: No RTC battery holder or header is documented or visible in the product overview.

form-factor-class.comment: Mixtile explicitly markets it as a "2.5-inch Pico-ITX form factor, 100 × 72 mm".

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Four corner mounting holes on a 100×72 mm PCB; not the Pi-standard 85×56 mm pattern.

weight-g.comment: Bare board weight is not published by Mixtile and is not stated in any of the reviews surveyed (CNX-Software, LinuxGizmos, Hackster, The DIY Life, magazinmehatronika).

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Board DT was added to upstream around 6.7; the Armbian images shipping today still default to the rockchip vendor 6.1 kernel because RK3588 GPU/NPU/video codec support upstream is partial. Boots mainline, but the AI/video accelerators that make this board interesting need vendor drivers.

official-os-image.comment: Ships with a customized Debian 11 image preloaded; vendor also provides Ubuntu and Android 12 images.

distros-with-images.comment: Armbian publishes Ubuntu 24.04 (GNOME and KDE) and Debian 13 minimal images for the board; Mixtile ships its own customized Debian image.

bootloader.comment: Standard Rockchip RK3588 boot chain: MaskROM → loader → U-Boot. The on-board dip switch selects boot media (eMMC / microSD / SPI flash / MaskROM).

long-term-support.comment: Mixtile does not publish a software-support window for Blade 3. User Manual was last refreshed 2024-05; vendor still patches images, but no formal LTS commitment.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Current Mixtile store base SKU (4 GB / 32 GB) as of 2026-04-26. Original Crowd Supply launch base price was $160 (2022).

msrp-top-usd.comment: Current Mixtile store top SKU (32 GB / 256 GB) as of 2026-04-26. Original Crowd Supply launch top price was $320 (2022).

available-regions.comment: Sold direct from Mixtile (Asia-Pacific), Crowd Supply (US, ships globally), ameriDroid (US), and Oz Robotics.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: ameriDroid lists both stocked SKUs as out of stock; Mixtile's own store still lists all four SKUs for sale. Typical resupply on direct orders runs to a few weeks.

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate: User Manual v1.3 with block diagram, pin assignments, and dimension drawings; online docs portal covers OS install and the 30-pin header. No public schematics or PCB design files.

community-size.comment: Active but small vendor-run forum; far smaller than Pi/Orange Pi/ODROID communities.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run Mixtile Community forum has a dedicated Blade 3 category.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Mixtile sells a Blade 3 Case (with U.2-to-M.2 NVMe adapter) and a Cluster Box that stacks four boards via U.2; otherwise minimal third-party accessories.
